reasoning: The document contains a total of 6 major defects between LLM fidelity reports and programmatic structural detectors, exceeding the threshold of 5 major defects for human review. These include broken/missing images, incorrect list nesting, displaced hyperlinks, table accessibility markup issues, and unsupported form elements. Because the major defect count exceeds 5, the document must be rejected.

concerns.analysis: The assessment section contains multiple-choice questions formatted as ordered lists with nested choices (a, b, c, d). However, the JSON output fails to consistently nest these sub-items properly. For example, in Question 3 (Page 58/59), the first option 'a. iron nail' is nested correctly as a child of the question, but the subsequent options ('b. penny', 'c. fingernail', 'd. all of the above') are extracted as sibling items to the main question. Similarly, in Question 14 (Page 60), all lettered
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
3. Which of the following could be used to test the hardness of a mineral?
a. iron nail
b. penny
c. fingernail
d. all of the above

concerns.analysis: [Downgraded: image_count_ratio=1.00, placeholder_ratio=0.00 contradict LLM claim] The source PDF contains several meaningful photographs of rocks and minerals used for a matching game on page 54 (items 5 and 6). In the HTML output, these images fail to render and are instead replaced by placeholder text `[Image: A collection of loose...]` and `[Image: A solid white rock...]`. The image manifest also confirms that these images have a base64 length of 0 and an unknown format, meaning the image data was not successfully extracted.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Photographs of rocks labeled '5' and '6' on page 54.
concerns.output_quote:
[Image: A collection of loose, small grey and brown rocks and gravel.]

[Image: A solid white rock or mineral specimen displayed next to a dark coin for size comparison.]

concerns.description: Table caption coded as header row: 3 table(s) have a single spanning first row that is likely a caption/title
concerns.analysis: caption_as_header=3. Examples: p28: "MOHS SCALE OF HARDNESS"; p35: "Mohs Hardness Scale With Examples of Common Tests"; p37: "Complete Mohs Hardness Scale With Examples of Common Tests". First row spans all columns — should be a <caption> element, not a header row.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 16 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=16, examples=['http://sargentwelch.com/sargent-welch-introductory-rock-coll', 'http://www.project2061.org/publications/bsl/online/bointro.h', 'http://sargentwelch.com/sargent-welch-introductory-rock-coll']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) have a data row marked as header: "Unit Title: Rocks and Minerals"
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=1. Tables use <th> for data values (misrepresenting data as headers) or have a data row mismarked as a header row. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

concerns.description: Form elements detected: 1 form(s) with 2 field(s) — forms lose functionality in HTML
concerns.analysis: form_count=1, total_fields=2. PDF form fields (checkboxes, text inputs, signatures) cannot be faithfully rendered as static HTML.
Text consistently appears as first or last element on 2/61 pages — likely a running header/footer
header_footer_count=58. Header/footer elements are present in extraction data. This is common in multi-page government PDFs and is typically cosmetic — the renderer strips them from HTML output.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 5 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=5. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.

action_items: Address list nesting for assessment multiple-choice questions
Ensure images are properly extracted with valid base64 data
Refactor table first rows to <caption> elements where appropriate
Fix displaced hyperlinks so they are inline with paragraph text
Correct table header markup for accessibility
Convert PDF form fields to functional HTML forms or static accessible equivalents
